subject: [PATCH v2 05/14] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch adds a new change_protection() flag, MM_CP_UFFD_RWP, which is used by userfaultfd (UFFD) to implement read-write protection. The flag installs a PAGE_NONE PTE with the uffd bit set, distinguishing it from other types of faults. This allows UFFD to protect pages from being accessed without its permission. The patch also introduces a new resolve flag, MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE, which restores the original page protection and clears the uffd bit. The changes are applied to various memory management functions, including change_pte_range(), change_huge_pmd(), and hugetlb_change_protection(). This patch is part of a larger series that aims to improve UFFD's functionality and security.
